name: Aeaea
description: Aeaea is a mythical island featured prominently in Homer's Odyssey. It is the home of the sorceress Circe, daughter of Helios, the sun god, and the Oceanid Perse. Aeaea is depicted as a lush and enchanting island, filled with dense forests, hidden coves, and opulent dwellings. It is a place of both beauty and danger, where mortals can be easily ensnared by Circe's magic and transformed into animals. Odysseus and his crew landed on Aeaea after escaping the Laestrygonians, and several of his men were turned into swine by Circe. Odysseus, aided by Hermes and the herb moly, was able to resist Circe's magic and eventually convinced her to restore his crew. They remained on the island for a year, enjoying Circe's hospitality and receiving valuable advice for their journey home. Aeaea represents the allure and peril of temptation, as well as the transformative power of both magic and adversity. The island's remote location and association with a powerful sorceress contribute to its mystique and enduring appeal in Greek mythology. Its role in the Odyssey highlights the challenges and temptations faced by heroes on their journeys, and the importance of cunning, resilience, and divine assistance in overcoming them. Aeaea serves as a potent symbol of the unknown and the potential dangers lurking in the uncharted waters of human experience.
subtitle: Home of the Sorceress Circe

keyMyths.title: Odysseus and Circe
keyMyths.description: Odysseus and his crew land on Aeaea, where Circe transforms some of his men into swine. Odysseus, aided by Hermes, resists her magic and convinces her to restore his crew.

keyMyths.title: Circe's Guidance
keyMyths.description: After a year on Aeaea, Circe provides Odysseus with crucial advice and warnings about the dangers he will face on his journey home, including the Sirens and Scylla and Charybdis.

sources.author: Homer
sources.description: Primary source describing Aeaea and its role in Odysseus's journey.
sources.text: Primary source describing Aeaea and its role in Odysseus's journey.
